name: Pythonic Patterns Assembly
description: Assemble idiomatic Python snippets with comprehensions and built-ins.

questions.question: Assemble list comprehension for squares of nums.
Assemble list comprehension for even values from nums.
questions.explanation: Comprehensions can include an inline if filter.

questions.question: Assemble dict comprehension mapping x to x squared.
Assemble set comprehension that lowercases words.
Assemble lambda that doubles x.
questions.explanation: lambda defines a small anonymous function.

questions.question: Assemble map call applying str to values.
questions.explanation: map applies a function over an iterable.

questions.question: Assemble filter call that keeps truthy values from vals.
Assemble for-loop using enumerate on items.
Assemble for-loop using zip over names and scores.
questions.explanation: zip iterates multiple lists in lockstep.

questions.question: Assemble sorted call with lowercase key.
questions.explanation: The key function controls sort comparison.

questions.question: Assemble swap assignment for a and b.
questions.explanation: Tuple unpacking swaps values without temp variable.

questions.question: Assemble starred unpacking to capture first and rest.
Assemble any() check for negative values in nums.
questions.explanation: any returns True if at least one element matches.

questions.question: Assemble all() check for positive values in nums.
questions.explanation: all returns True only if every element matches.

questions.question: Assemble expression that builds list from map output.
questions.explanation: Wrap map with list to eagerly realize results.
